title: Creamy Porridge

overview.description: A simple, creamy bowl of porridge made with rolled oats and milk, cooked gently until smooth and comforting.

method: Combine the oats, water, milk and salt in a saucepan.
Bring slowly to a gentle simmer over a medium heat.
Cook for 8–10 minutes, stirring regularly until thick and creamy.
Serve immediately with your choice of toppings.

learning.did-you-know: Adding a little water as well as milk helps the oats cook more evenly while still producing a rich, creamy porridge.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to cook rolled oats gently to achieve a creamy texture without sticking or scorching.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: An inexpensive, filling breakfast made from pantry staples that can be varied with seasonal fruit and different toppings.
chapman-tips: A pinch of salt makes a surprising difference to the flavour.
Cook gently rather than boiling hard for the creamiest texture.
Add a splash of milk at the end if the porridge becomes thicker than you like.
Rolled oats give the best balance of texture and cooking time.
Stir raisins into the porridge during the final 2 minutes of cooking so they plump up and sweeten the porridge naturally.
notes.leftovers: Reheat with a splash of milk to loosen the texture.
